content.text: SOC 2 Control Mapping for GenAI Operations

SOC 2 (System and Organisation Controls 2) reports provide independent assurance to customers and regulators that a service organisation's controls meet the AICPA's Trust Service Criteria (TSC). For organisations that operate GenAI systems — whether as a service or internally — a SOC 2 Type II report is increasingly expected by enterprise procurement teams and regulators. This sub-subtopic covers how the five Trust Service Criteria map to GenAI-specific controls and what evidence an auditor will seek.
: a SOC 2 Type I report attests that controls are
at a point in time. A Type II report attests that controls
over an audit period (typically 6–12 months). Enterprise customers almost always require Type II.
Trust Service Criteria Mapped to GenAI Controls

attrs.alt: SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria mapped to GenAI controls diagram

content.text: Security (CC) is the only mandatory TSC. The other four are selected based on the nature of the service. For a GenAI API or platform, selecting all five is common:
Key GenAI additions beyond standard scope
Mandatory; covers access control, change management, vendor risk, and incident response
Prompt injection controls; model version change management; LLM vendor TPRM
LLM inference APIs have latency and uptime SLAs that customers rely on
Rate limiting; graceful degradation to fallback model; canary probes
Prompts frequently contain confidential business data; vendor training-data risk
PII output filter; no-training clause enforcement; prompt log minimisation
Prompts often contain personal data subject to GDPR/CCPA
Output PII filter; DSAR deletion workflow for prompt data; no-training contract clause
Hallucinations and model drift affect the accuracy of AI-generated outputs
Confidence-score gate; HITL review for high-stakes outputs; hallucination detection

GENAI_SOC2_MAP = SOC2ControlMap(mappings=[

    # ── SECURITY (CC) — mandatory ────────────────────────────────────────────
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="CC6.1",
        category=SOC2Category.CC,
        description="Logical access controls restrict access to system components.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "Role-based access control (RBAC) on the inference API gateway. "
            "Service accounts for each use case; no shared credentials. "
            "Access reviewed quarterly."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-RBAC-001", "CTR-RBAC-002"],
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="CC6.7",
        category=SOC2Category.CC,
        description="Data in transit is protected.",
        genai_implementation="TLS 1.2+ enforced on all inference API calls; mTLS for internal services.",
        controls=["CTR-TLS-001"],
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="CC7.2",
        category=SOC2Category.CC,
        description="Security events and anomalies are monitored and assessed.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "Prompt anomaly monitor flags statistical outliers in prompt length, "
            "vocabulary, and topic distribution. Alerts within 5 minutes."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-MON-001", "CTR-MON-002"],
        ai_specific=True,   # standard CC7.2 covers log monitoring; AI extends to prompt anomalies
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="CC8.1",
        category=SOC2Category.CC,
        description="Changes to system components are authorised and documented.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "Model version changes require two-person approval in the model registry. "
            "Automated rollback if post-deployment canary tests fail."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-CHG-001", "CTR-CHG-002"],
        ai_specific=True,
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="CC9.2",
        category=SOC2Category.CC,
        description="Vendor and business partner risk is assessed and managed.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "LLM vendor TPRM assessment at onboarding + annual re-assessment. "
            "Continuous monitoring for cert expiry and security incidents."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-TPRM-001", "CTR-TPRM-002"],
    ),

    # ── AVAILABILITY (A) — selected ──────────────────────────────────────────
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="A1.1",
        category=SOC2Category.A,
        description="Availability commitments and system requirements are communicated.",
        genai_implementation="Inference API SLA (99.5% uptime) published in service catalogue; monitored via canary probes.",
        controls=["CTR-SLA-001"],
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="A1.2",
        category=SOC2Category.A,
        description="Environmental threats are identified and managed.",
        genai_implementation="Multi-region failover for inference gateway; fallback to secondary LLM vendor if primary unavailable.",
        controls=["CTR-RES-001", "CTR-RES-002"],
        ai_specific=True,
    ),

    # ── CONFIDENTIALITY (C) — selected ───────────────────────────────────────
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="C1.2",
        category=SOC2Category.C,
        description="Confidential information is protected during processing and transmission.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "PII output filter masks SSN, credit card, and health identifiers before response delivery. "
            "Prompt/response logs store SHA-256 hashes only; raw text not persisted."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-PII-001", "CTR-LOG-001"],
        ai_specific=True,
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="C1.3",
        category=SOC2Category.C,
        description="Confidential information is disposed of when no longer needed.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "Inference logs retained 3 years then deleted per retention schedule. "
            "No-training clause enforced with LLM vendor; verified annually."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-RET-001", "CTR-TPRM-001"],
    ),

    # ── PRIVACY (P) — selected ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="P4.1",
        category=SOC2Category.P,
        description="Personal information is used only for its stated purpose.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "Prompts containing personal data are processed only for the stated use case (e.g. customer support). "
            "Contractual prohibition on vendor using prompt data for model training."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-PRI-001", "CTR-TPRM-001"],
    ),
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="P8.1",
        category=SOC2Category.P,
        description="Data subject access requests are fulfilled.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "DSAR workflow can retrieve and delete prompt-level data by user ID. "
            "Inference log index supports user_id queries for DSAR fulfilment."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-PRI-002"],
    ),

    # ── PROCESSING INTEGRITY (PI) — selected ─────────────────────────────────
    SOC2Criterion(
        criterion_id="PI1.2",
        category=SOC2Category.PI,
        description="System processing is complete, accurate, timely, and authorised.",
        genai_implementation=(
            "Confidence-score gate rejects responses below threshold 0.75. "
            "HITL review required for all flagged high-stakes outputs. "
            "Hallucination detection alerts if RAG-retrieved facts diverge from response."
        ),
        controls=["CTR-CONF-001", "CTR-HITL-001", "CTR-HALL-001"],
        ai_specific=True,
    ),
])

def generate_soc2_coverage_report(
    control_map: SOC2ControlMap,
    implemented_controls: set[str],
) -> dict:
    """
    Produces a SOC 2 coverage report: for each criterion, is it satisfied
    by at least one implemented control?
    """
    results = {}
    for criterion in control_map.mappings:
        satisfying = [c for c in criterion.controls if c in implemented_controls]
        results[criterion.criterion_id] = {
            "category":       criterion.category.value,
            "description":    criterion.description[:80] + "...",
            "required_controls": criterion.controls,
            "implemented":    satisfying,
            "satisfied":      len(satisfying) > 0,
            "ai_specific":    criterion.ai_specific,
        }

    satisfied   = sum(1 for r in results.values() if r["satisfied"])
    unsatisfied = sum(1 for r in results.values() if not r["satisfied"])
    return {
        "criteria_total":       len(results),
        "criteria_satisfied":   satisfied,
        "criteria_unsatisfied": unsatisfied,
        "coverage_pct":         round(satisfied / len(results) * 100, 1),
        "detail":               results,
    }

# Usage:
implemented = {"CTR-RBAC-001", "CTR-TLS-001", "CTR-MON-001", "CTR-MON-002",
               "CTR-CHG-001", "CTR-TPRM-001", "CTR-PII-001", "CTR-HITL-001"}
report = generate_soc2_coverage_report(GENAI_SOC2_MAP, implemented)
print(f"SOC 2 coverage: {report['coverage_pct']}% "
      f"({report['criteria_satisfied']}/{report['criteria_total']} criteria satisfied)")
The Common Criteria (CC) subcategories most relevant to GenAI operations:
RBAC on inference API; per-use-case service accounts; quarterly access review
IAM policy export; access review sign-off records
TLS 1.2+ on all external calls; mTLS for internal inference pipeline
TLS config export; network scan results
Prompt anomaly monitor (statistical outliers in vocabulary/length/topic); alert within 5 minutes
Alert log; monitor config; false-positive rate metrics
Two-person approval in model registry; automated rollback on canary failure
Model registry deployment log with approver IDs; rollback incident record if applicable
LLM vendor TPRM at onboarding + annual; continuous cert monitoring
TPRM assessment report; vendor cert tracker export
PI1 (complete, accurate, timely, authorised processing) is where GenAI systems face the most audit scrutiny that standard IT systems do not. Auditors will probe:

content: : is every inference request logged? Is there evidence that no requests are silently dropped?
: what controls prevent the system from delivering hallucinated or factually incorrect outputs? How is the accuracy threshold defined and enforced?
: for high-stakes outputs (medical, legal, financial), who authorises the AI to act autonomously vs requiring human review?
: is there a latency SLA? What happens when the model exceeds it?

content.text:
@dataclass
class PIComplianceEvidence:
    """Evidence package for PI1 criterion — assembled for SOC 2 audit."""
    audit_period_start: date
    audit_period_end: date

    # PI1.1 — Completeness
    total_inference_requests: int
    total_log_records: int
    daily_count_checksums: list[dict]   # [{date, request_count, log_count, match: bool}]

    # PI1.2 — Accuracy
    confidence_gate_config: dict        # threshold value and version
    gate_rejection_rate: float          # % of requests rejected by confidence gate
    hitl_review_rate: float             # % escalated to human review
    hallucination_alert_count: int

    # PI1.3 — Authorisation
    high_stakes_use_cases: list[str]    # use cases where HITL is mandatory
    hitl_approval_log_count: int        # records in HITL decision log
    unapproved_high_stakes_count: int   # should be 0

    # PI1.4 — Timeliness
    p99_latency_ms: float
    sla_threshold_ms: float
    sla_breach_count: int
    uptime_pct: float

    def pi_summary(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "completeness_match": self.total_inference_requests == self.total_log_records,
            "accuracy_gate_active": self.confidence_gate_config.get("enabled", False),
            "authorisation_clean": self.unapproved_high_stakes_count == 0,
            "sla_met": self.p99_latency_ms <= self.sla_threshold_ms,
            "uptime_met": self.uptime_pct >= 99.5,
        }
Scope Decisions for the SOC 2 Report
Before engaging an auditor, define what is in and out of scope. For GenAI operations, typical scope decisions include:
Primary system boundary; all controls implemented here
LLM vendor API (e.g. third-party model provider)
Vendor provides their own SOC 2; reference as a subservice with user entity controls
IN scope if managed in-house; OUT if vendor-managed
Depends on who controls the training environment
Contains customer-facing data; access and accuracy controls apply
Change management controls for model versions are in scope
Detective controls rely on this system
Common SOC 2 Gaps in GenAI Operations
No change management for model updates

content: : deploying a new model version without the same approval rigour as a software release is the most common CC8.1 gap. Every model version change is a system change.

: CC9.2 requires you to assess your LLM vendor's controls. Simply obtaining their SOC 2 report is not sufficient — you must read the user entity controls section and verify you have implemented the required compensating controls.
: a PI1.2 finding arises when the confidence threshold exists in code but is not documented in a formal control specification with an owner and an approved threshold value.
: auditors testing PI1.3 will sample HITL records and read rationale fields. Empty rationale fields are a finding.
: a C1.2 gap arises when the PII filter exists but there are no control test results proving it fires correctly in the audit period.

content.text: SOC 2 for GenAI operations requires satisfying all five Trust Service Criteria: the mandatory Security criteria (CC) and the selected Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy, and Processing Integrity criteria. The GenAI-specific additions — prompt anomaly monitoring, model version change management, confidence-score gating, HITL review, and PII output filtering — extend standard controls to cover risks unique to probabilistic AI systems. Build a bidirectional control map from TSC criterion to control ID so that evidence collection for each criterion can be automated, and run a coverage report before audit fieldwork to confirm no criterion is left without an implemented, tested control.
